title: Omitting requested_models bypasses a tenant model allowlist

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: IssueReq defaults requested_models to an empty vector.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The subset check is vacuously satisfied when the request omits models.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The empty request vector is passed unchanged into the token issuance request.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: compose_token copies the empty vector into TrustToken.allowed_models.

affected_locations.label: semantic_control
affected_locations.detail: Token attenuation explicitly defines an empty allowed_models vector as unrestricted/all models.

source: An authenticated tenant principal can POST /v1/tokens/issue with requested_models omitted or [] (lib.rs:113-125,327-331).
closest_control: policy.allows_model is called for each requested model at lib.rs:389-400, but there is no nonempty/default-to-policy branch, so an empty request bypasses the control.
sink_broken_control: The handler passes the empty vector to IssueTokenRequest.with_models at lib.rs:424-426 and the bridge signs it into allowed_models at crates/wsf-bridge/src/lib.rs:324-341; empty means unrestricted at crates/fabric-token/src/lib.rs:547-555.
impact: A tenant policy restricted to selected models can issue a token representing all-model authority, enabling access to models the policy intended to exclude wherever TrustToken.allowed_models is enforced according to its documented empty-is-unrestricted semantics.
plausibility: Direct authenticated route-to-signed-token data flow; no configuration mismatch or forged token is required.

candidate_local_validation.method: Static branch/data-flow proof with semantic cross-check in fabric-token
candidate_local_validation.evidence: For a nonempty policy.allowed_models and an omitted request field, the loop at lib.rs:390 executes zero iterations, then lines 424-426 preserve [], bridge lines 340 copy [], and fabric-token lines 548-555 state [] is unrestricted.

candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: policy-restricted tenant -> empty request -> unrestricted signed token -> downstream model authorization bypass
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Requires valid tenant authentication but crosses a server-side authorization boundary and creates reusable signed authority.

title: Attenuation ignores the tenant maximum delegation depth

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Tenant policy declares max_delegation_depth as a chain-level bound.

affected_locations.label: closest_control
affected_locations.detail: Issuance only distinguishes zero from nonzero depth and does not bind the numeric limit into the token or lineage.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: The reachable attenuation handler always passes max_child_depth=None.

affected_locations.label: sink/root_control
affected_locations.detail: narrow_child rejects only Some(0); None permits a child and the child stores only parent_id, not remaining depth.

source: An authenticated tenant principal can repeatedly submit each signed child as the parent to /v1/tokens/attenuate.
closest_control: The policy gate at lib.rs:367-375 rejects only a policy value of zero during initial issuance; the attenuation call passes None at lib.rs:541 and no lineage-depth lookup occurs.
sink_broken_control: fabric_token::attenuate signs each child after narrow_child, whose only depth check is matches!(Some(0)) at fabric-token/src/lib.rs:510-512.
impact: A tenant configured for a finite delegation depth (for example 1 or 3) can create arbitrarily deep valid delegation chains, defeating bounded delegation and extending authority beyond policy intent.
plausibility: The same endpoint accepts a child token as a parent, signature and tenant checks pass, and no remaining-depth state is stored or consulted.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Policy max_delegation_depth is read only at initial issuance (rg found policy.rs:66, lib.rs:367, and tests); attenuation passes None and fabric-token only rejects Some(0). Repeating child-as-parent therefore never reaches DepthExceeded.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: Authenticated caller holding any delegation-capable token under a policy with max_delegation_depth > 0
candidate_local_attack_path.closest_control: Initial zero/nonzero gate plus max_child_depth API
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Bridge signer signs a new child on every hop
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: finite-depth policy -> root token -> child1 -> child2 -> unbounded descendants
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: No authority widening per hop is needed; the violated invariant is bounded delegation lineage and durable authority propagation.

title: A token revoked after issuance can still be attenuated into a new signed child

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Attenuation builds a VerificationContext without fresh revocation state.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: VerificationContext defaults require_fresh_revocation=false and revocation=None.

affected_locations.label: broken_control
affected_locations.detail: verify_in_context consults current revocation only if a snapshot was supplied; otherwise a signed Valid status remains accepted.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The accepted parent is narrowed and re-signed under a caller-selected new token id.

affected_locations.label: revocation_predicate
affected_locations.detail: Token-id revocation checks the current token_id, so a newly minted child id is not covered by a parent-id-only revocation.

source: A previously valid signed parent token whose token_id has since been added to the current revocation snapshot.
closest_control: VerificationContext verifies signature, time, tenant, and bundle but the handler neither calls require_fresh_revocation/with_revocation nor stores revocation state in AppState.
sink_broken_control: fabric_token::attenuate signs a child with a new token_id after accepting the stale parent.
impact: A holder can race or act after token-id revocation to mint a new valid child not matched by the parent token-id revocation, bypassing revocation and restoring usable authority.
plausibility: The behavior follows directly from context defaults and the revocation predicate; subject/tenant/key-wide revocations would still catch the child, but ordinary token-id revocation would not.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: API lines 526-535 do not attach a snapshot; VerificationContext defaults at fabric-token lines 228-231; child token_id is replaced at fabric-token lines 515-524; RevocationSnapshot::revokes checks token.token_id, not parent_id, at fabric-revocation lines 150-167.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: Holder of a token valid at issuance but revoked by token_id in current state
candidate_local_attack_path.closest_control: Signed embedded revocation_status plus optional context snapshot
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Trust bridge signer emits child with new token_id
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: revoked parent -> stale verification -> new child id -> revocation miss -> renewed signed authority
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Requires possession of a formerly valid token; token-id revocation is bypassed while broader tenant/subject/key revocation remains effective.

title: The verify endpoint reports stale-revoked tokens as valid

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: The verify handler checks only fabric_token::verify and expiry before returning valid=true.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: fabric_token::verify checks the signed embedded revocation_status but has no current snapshot input.

affected_locations.label: state_contract
affected_locations.detail: AppState exposes no revocation store for the verify handler.

source: Any authenticated WSF principal can submit a correctly signed, unexpired token that was revoked after issuance.
closest_control: The signed revocation_status rejects tokens already stamped Revoked, but it cannot reflect later server-side revocation and no current snapshot is consulted.
sink_broken_control: VerifyResp.valid is returned true at lib.rs:501-505 for the stale token.
impact: A consumer treating /v1/tokens/verify as an authorization oracle can continue accepting a revoked token.
plausibility: The response behavior is deterministic from the code and contradicts the current-revocation invariant, but the reviewed repository path does not show a concrete downstream consumer making an authorization decision solely from VerifyResp.valid.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: A signed token with revocation_status=Valid passes fabric_token::verify even if a later snapshot revokes it; if unexpired, lib.rs:501-505 returns valid=true.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No concrete in-repository authorization consumer of the REST VerifyResp was found in the minimum supporting search; final reportability depends on deployed consumer semantics.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: Previously valid token revoked only in current server-side snapshot
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: stale token -> verification oracle says valid -> external consumer may authorize
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Behavior is reachable and wrong for freshness, but privilege impact is consumer-dependent.

title: The shipped seal operation accepts tokens revoked after issuance

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: The authenticated seal handler tenant-binds the token then delegates to SealService.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: SealService::new defaults revocation to None; with_revocation_store is optional.

affected_locations.label: broken_control
affected_locations.detail: check_revocation returns Ok immediately when no store is configured.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: SealService performs OpenBao login, generates a data key, and invokes Transit after the optional check.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: The shipped API binary constructs SealService::new without with_revocation_store.

source: An authenticated principal presents a validly signed, unexpired same-tenant token that has since been revoked in current revocation state.
closest_control: SealService verifies signature/expiry and has a fail-closed snapshot path only when a store is explicitly wired; the shipped construction leaves it absent.
sink_broken_control: The revoked caller reaches OpenBao-backed envelope sealing and service provenance signing.
impact: Revoked authority can continue exercising privileged cryptographic sealing and generating trusted envelopes/receipts until token expiry.
plausibility: Exact runtime construction and fail-open None branch are present in the shipped wsf-api binary.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: main.rs:185-192 calls SealService::new only; wsf-seal:291-293 returns Ok without a store; seal then reaches OpenBao Transit at wsf-seal:322-335.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: Holder of a later-revoked same-tenant token
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: OpenBao Transit encryption plus signed envelope provenance
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: revoked token -> absent store -> privileged sealing operation
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Same-tenant and valid-signature prerequisites remain; the issue defeats revocation for a cryptographic authority operation.

title: The shipped unseal operation accepts tokens revoked after issuance

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: The authenticated unseal handler tenant-binds the token then returns recovered plaintext.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: SealService::new defaults revocation to None; with_revocation_store is optional.

affected_locations.label: broken_control
affected_locations.detail: check_revocation returns Ok immediately when no store is configured.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: After token and envelope binding checks, unseal invokes OpenBao Transit decrypt and recovers plaintext.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: The shipped API binary constructs SealService::new without with_revocation_store.

source: An authenticated principal presents a validly signed, unexpired same-tenant token that has since been revoked in current revocation state.
closest_control: Signature, expiry, envelope tenant/audience, classification, and permitted-op checks are present, but the current revocation gate is optional and absent in the shipped binary.
sink_broken_control: OpenBao Transit decrypt and plaintext return remain reachable for a revoked token.
impact: A revoked tenant subject/token can continue decrypting same-tenant protected envelopes and recovering regulated plaintext until token expiry.
plausibility: Exact runtime construction, fail-open None branch, and decrypt sink are present; no broader compromise is required beyond possession of the formerly valid token.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: main.rs:185-192 omits with_revocation_store; wsf-seal:291-293 accepts absent state; wsf-seal:375-430 proceeds to Transit decrypt after other authorization checks.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: Holder of a later-revoked same-tenant token and an envelope
candidate_local_attack_path.closest_control: Optional revocation store plus correct tenant/clearance checks
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: OpenBao Transit decrypt and plaintext response
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: revoked token -> absent current state -> envelope decrypt -> regulated-data disclosure
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Same-tenant envelope and clearance are required, but revocation is specifically intended to terminate that existing access.

title: The shipped AWS credential exchange accepts tokens revoked after issuance

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: The exchange handler resolves a tenant-scoped grant then calls AwsStsBroker.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: AWS exchange calls verify_token then OpenBao and STS.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: AwsStsBroker::new defaults revocation to None; wiring is optional.

affected_locations.label: broken_control
affected_locations.detail: verify_token returns Ok when revocation is None.

affected_locations.label: runtime_wrapper
affected_locations.detail: The shipped API constructs AwsStsBroker::new without with_revocation_store.

source: An authenticated principal with a validly signed, unexpired same-tenant token and approved grant after that token/subject/tenant is revoked in current state.
closest_control: Signature, expiry, tenant grant binding, scoped IAM policy, and optional snapshot checks exist; the shipped broker omits the snapshot store and the helper explicitly succeeds without it.
sink_broken_control: The broker fetches root credentials from OpenBao and calls AWS STS AssumeRole for the revoked caller.
impact: Revoked authority can mint fresh cloud credentials for an approved role and retain cloud access despite revocation.
plausibility: Concrete shipped construction and fail-open None branch reach the live OpenBao/STS path.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: main.rs:180-184 constructs the broker without the builder; broker lib.rs:324-326 returns Ok for None; broker lib.rs:217-233 then obtains root credentials and assumes the role.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: Holder of a later-revoked same-tenant token plus an approved grant_id
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: revoked token -> absent revocation state -> root credential use -> fresh cloud credentials
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Requires a preexisting approved grant, but revocation should terminate precisely this standing authorization; cloud credentials can persist independently after exchange.

title: AWS exchange extends near-expiry token authority to the 15-minute STS floor

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: The credential exchange endpoint returns brokered AWS credentials.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: The broker verifies the token, computes duration, and calls STS.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The token remaining lifetime is passed to clamp_duration using a 900-second floor.

affected_locations.label: broken_control
affected_locations.detail: remaining.clamp(min,max) raises any positive remaining lifetime below min to min instead of refusing exchange.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The widened duration_secs is supplied to AWS STS AssumeRole.

source: An authenticated caller exchanges a valid token with less than 900 seconds remaining.
closest_control: verify_token rejects only already-expired tokens at lib.rs:317-323; it does not require remaining lifetime to meet the STS floor.
sink_broken_control: clamp_duration widens the remaining TTL to 900 seconds and STS returns credentials that outlive the authorizing token.
impact: A caller can exchange immediately before token expiry and retain cloud-role access for roughly 15 additional minutes, defeating token expiry and expanding the revocation/compromise window.
plausibility: Deterministic arithmetic in the deployed AWS path; AWS minimum duration is encoded as 900 seconds by BrokerConfig::new at lib.rs:53-67.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: For remaining=1, Rust i64::clamp(900,3600) returns 900; the already-expired guard does not fire and duration_secs=900 reaches STS at lib.rs:228.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: Valid token with 1-899 seconds remaining and an approved grant
candidate_local_attack_path.closest_control: Expiry check and intended TTL clamp
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: near-expiry token -> floor widening -> credentials outlive token -> continued cloud access
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Bounded to the STS minimum window and an approved grant, but directly defeats freshness/expiry semantics at a cloud credential boundary.

title: TenantIssuancePolicy.max_classification is not enforced when a token is issued

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Policy declares max_classification as the highest classification a token may carry.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Issuance applies modes, roles, models, and budget but never reads policy.max_classification.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: compose_token stamps max_data_classification solely from OpenBao TenantAttributes.

affected_locations.label: runtime_configuration
affected_locations.detail: The shipped binary currently uses single_dev policy whose classification is Restricted, limiting demonstrated mismatch impact.

source: Any authenticated principal receiving a token where the OpenBao tenant classification is higher than the API TenantIssuancePolicy.max_classification.
closest_control: Tenant policy lookup succeeds and other axes are checked, but max_classification is never compared, intersected, or passed to the bridge.
sink_broken_control: The bridge signs the OpenBao classification directly into TrustToken.max_data_classification, which downstream unseal authorization uses as clearance.
impact: A policy intended to cap a tenant below its OpenBao authorization envelope can issue over-cleared tokens that decrypt data above the API policy ceiling.
plausibility: The field is a documented security control and is dead on the issuance path, but the shipped main currently builds a Restricted single-dev policy; a concrete deployed lower-policy/higher-OpenBao mismatch was not found in the minimum supporting files.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Repository search found max_classification declarations/tests but no read in the issue handler; bridge lines 324-341 source classification from OpenBao only.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Need a concrete production TenantPolicyStore/configuration with max_classification below the corresponding OpenBao TenantAttributes value to establish deployed exploitability.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: Authenticated tenant principal under mismatched server policy sources
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Signed over-cleared TrustToken consumed by unseal clearance check
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: policy-source mismatch -> unchecked higher OpenBao classification -> signed token -> high-classification unseal
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Requires operator/config mismatch or an alternate TenantPolicyStore; default shipped single-dev policy is already Restricted.
